question.text: An employee withdraws Rs. 3,50,000 from his EPF account after 4 years of continuous service due to resignation. He has furnished his PAN. What is the TDS applicable on the withdrawal?

answer.text: TDS at 10% on Rs. 3,50,000 = Rs. 35,000, since withdrawal is before 5 years and exceeds Rs. 50,000 with PAN furnished
No TDS since EPF withdrawals are always tax-free
TDS at 10% on Rs. 3,00,000 (amount above Rs. 50,000 threshold)
TDS at maximum marginal rate since the withdrawal is premature

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** EPF withdrawal before 5 years of continuous service is taxable. When the amount exceeds Rs. 50,000 and PAN is furnished, TDS is deducted at 10% on the entire withdrawal amount (not just the excess over Rs. 50,000). The Rs. 50,000 is a threshold for TDS applicability, not an exemption amount. TDS at maximum marginal rate (MMR) applies only when PAN is NOT furnished.
**The Trap:** Two traps here: (1) Thinking EPF is always tax-free — it is EEE only after 5 years of continuous service. (2) Applying TDS only on the amount exceeding Rs. 50,000 — the Rs. 50,000 is a trigger threshold, not a deduction. Once exceeded, TDS applies on the full amount.
**Cross-Exam Context:** EPF taxation on premature withdrawal is tested in NISM X-A, X-B, and is a staple in income tax papers. The interaction between PAN furnishing and TDS rate is also tested in KYC-related sections.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always advise clients to complete 5 years before withdrawing EPF if possible. If they must withdraw early, ensure PAN is linked to avoid MMR taxation. Also note exceptions: ill health, employer business closure, and transfer to new employer's EPF don't attract tax even before 5 years.
